title: SSRF Defense, Prototype-Safe Data, Terminal Injection, Paths, and Resource Limits · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for ssrf defense, prototype-safe data, terminal injection, paths, and resource limits and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, compile diagnostics, explicit non-claims, and named browser, TypeScript, Node, network, filesystem, terminal, load, accessibility, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for deny-by-default TypeScript trust-boundary policy. Learners progress through…
objectives: Revalidate scheme, hostname, port, path class, and redirect count for every destination and reject user-controlled egress.
Use own-property checks, explicit field construction, safe records, and no blind assignment from unknown objects.
Normalize or visibly escape control, ANSI, bidi, and non-printing sequences from commands, API payloads, and files.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex Ts Redirect Policy · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript team handles case 4345 during an offline mobile-library catalog migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce redirect, prototype, terminal, path, privacy, and resource boundaries; unknown external values must not cross the runtime validator; then change one command, token, EOF, abort, or output-width case and preserve the session model. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
